quality_hard_gates.description: Candidate must include profile, parts binding/inventory, per-component layout guide packet, source map summary, citation map, figure/table extraction summary, extraction manifest, rejected visual/table candidates, and boundary/readiness review.
Candidate must not claim actual network access, browser use, file writes, downloads, PDF rendering, OCR, Camelot, PyMuPDF, local project reads, or credential/session use.
Candidate must avoid host-specific absolute paths and concrete run ids. The portable binding `_workspaces/<project_code>/reference_materials/from_exp_xml/parts` is allowed.
Each layout finding for U1 and U2 must cite source_file, source_file_sha256, page_number, and span_id or bounded_excerpt_anchor.
U1 must preserve source-backed decoupling/power placement, thermal/exposed-pad/via/ground, and EVAL/reference-layout findings.
U2 must reuse the datasheet cache, record local EVAL none_found, include the approved mocked supplemental app note, and cite the supplemental page for final layout readiness.
J1 must remain review_required with no invented datasheets, EVAL material, figures, tables, or layout guidance.

Candidate must map final layout guide citations to unique source-file-checksum plus page pairs, dedupe them, and promote only cited full-page figures directly under Layout Guide/figures. Non-cited/context/legacy items must stay separate.
Candidate must promote only board/layout-context tables with quality metrics and reject ordering tables, revision history, setup-only, marketing, and non-layout tables.

Candidate must record processed docs, cache hit/miss behavior, candidate span count, supplemental docs, figure/table counts, rejected candidates, warnings/open questions, and ready vs blocked components.

All findings, promoted figures/tables, source decisions, and readiness conclusions must be derivable only from fixture-provided spans, mocked source metadata, visual/table candidate records, and approved supplemental records.

Candidate must not reproduce copied vendor text, non-synthetic source content, customer/project data, secrets, credentials, cookies, or anything outside the provided synthetic summaries, anchors, mocked metadata, and candidate identifiers.
Only fixture-approved supplemental sources may be incorporated. URL-only, unapproved, or invented supplemental references must remain gaps or review items.
Any component without sufficient cited local or approved supplemental evidence must remain blocked or review_required. Readiness cannot be inferred from generic component knowledge.

Every finding, promoted figure/table, rejection reason, and readiness conclusion must trace to the same component/refdes and to that component's fixture spans, approved supplemental spans, or visual/table candidate metadata.
Promotion and rejection records must include evidence-based reasons, not only candidate ids, dispositions, or aggregate counts.
Candidates may report fixture metadata such as mocked URLs, checksums, cache status, and mock_saved state, but must phrase them as synthetic fixture facts rather than performed runtime operations.

signals: runner can write Markdown/JSON/manifests from packet
lower output tokens among quality-passing candidates
lower reasoning tokens when quality is comparable
lower wall time among quality-passing candidates
passes simple rule checks with margin
no brittle reliance on wording from golden

semantic_scoring_policy.required_output_shape: Score by semantic presence of required packet data, not exact heading names or ordering.
semantic_scoring_policy.component_content: Score U1 and U2 by coverage of source-backed topics and citation correctness, not golden wording.
semantic_scoring_policy.figure_table_handling: Score promotion/rejection reasoning and evidence discipline. Exact path strings matter only when they express the workflow contract, such as current cited full-page figures belonging directly under Layout Guide/figures.
semantic_scoring_policy.readiness_wording: Exact readiness wording is not required; evidence-backed state and rationale matter.

measurement_note: This run uses a CLI-only staged fallback because the current runtime does not permit the default subagent_quality_first matrix. CLI usage is exact for the CLI runs, but not comparable to unavailable subagent-runner telemetry.
